title: AchievementsExpandWorldborder
description: A simple Minecraft plugin that implements "Achievements increase world border size" mechanic
body:
# AchievementsExpandWorldborder
A simple Bukkit plugin that expands the worldborder when new Achievements / Advancements are unlocked. Works with [BlazeandCave's Advancements Datapack](https://www.planetminecraft.com/data-pack/blazeandcave-s-advancements-pack-1-12/)

## Why did I create this?
I was looking for a similar project to this, but couldn't find any that worked, so I just decided to make one myself

## How to use
Since the world border doesn't activate before you gain an advancement, here's a short guide on how to start your world.

0. Install [BlazeandCave's Advancements Datapack](https://www.planetminecraft.com/data-pack/blazeandcave-s-advancements-pack-1-12/). While **this isn't mandatory**, it makes the experience much better
1. Find a good place for your spawn.
2. Run `/worldborder center ~ ~ ~`
3. Get your first advancement, either using `/advancement grant`, or by getting one normally. 
4. Change the world border size if needed using /amount

## Commands
/amount <number>
> Changes how many blocks the world border increases per achievement. Supports decimals (such as 0.5)

/sync
> Synchronizes the world border size with the world border amount.

/advancements
> Get the amount of advancements unlocked

/settings
> Change a setting

## Settings
To view up-to-date settings, run `/settings` to view available settings, and their description

## Permissions
`wb.set`
> Allows users to change the worldborder expand amount

`wb.sync`
> Allows the use of the /sync command

`wb.advancement`
> Allows users to view the number of advancements 

`wb.settings`
> Allows user to modify settings


## Version support
The plugin should work on any Minecraft version which uses java 16 or later. The plugin has been tested on 1.21.4, 1.20.4, and 1.16.5
